channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Homeland Security ends TSA collective bargaining agreement, in effort to dismantle union protections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Department of Homeland Security said Friday that it is ending the collective bargaining agreement with the tens of thousands of frontline employees at the Transportation Security Administration, marking a major effort to dismantle union protections under the Trump administratio
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Expert tells judge to drop charges against NYC Mayor Eric Adams without letting them be refiled
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge has no choice but to grant the Justice Department’s unusual and divisive request to dismiss New York City Mayor Eric Adams’ corruption case, a court-appointed lawyer said Friday.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California and Texas join push to end remote work among state employees
item.description: Hundreds of thousands of state employees across the U.S. are being ordered back into the office as governors move to shadow President Trump’s mandate for federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration throws hundreds of affordable housing projects into limbo after contract cuts
item.description: The Trump administration has stalled at least $60 million in funding intended largely for affordable housing developments nationwide, throwing hundreds of projects into a precarious limbo, according to information and documents obtained by The Associated Press The move is part of a flurry of funding
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: State Dept using AI to ban visas over perceived pro-Hamas posts
item.description: The initiative, started under Secretary of State Marco Rubio, will use AI to scan student visa holders’ social media accounts.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Louisiana student died after being punched in hazing ritual, police say
item.description: Baton Rouge police confirmed Southern University student Caleb Wilson died after being punched in the chest multiple times during a hazing ritual. One arrest has been made, and more suspects are expected.
item.dc:creator: Keymonte Avery, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexican cartel leader’s son is sentenced to life in prison for role in major drug trafficking plot
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The son of a Mexican drug cartel boss was sentenced Friday to life in prison for helping his father run one of the country’s largest and most violent narcotics trafficking organizations.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tony! Toni! Tone! guitarist D'Wayne Wiggins dies
item.description: D'Wayne Wiggins, guitarist and founding member of Tony! Toni! Tone!, has died, according to a social media post from his family. The guitarist, who was a native of Oakland, had been battling bladder cancer.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: States sue President Trump’s administration over mass firings of probationary federal workers
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Maryland and 19 other states are suing multiple federal agencies, contending President Donald Trump's administration has illegally fired thousands of federal probationary workers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens tariffs on Canadian dairy, lumber products as soon as Friday
item.description: Access to dairy and lumber markets have long been two of the biggest points of contention in the otherwise strong economic relationship between the U.S. and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: 20 Dem AGs sue over Trump efforts to fire probationary employees
item.description: Democratic state attorneys general in Washington, D.C., and 19 states sued the Trump administration late Thursday over its efforts to carry out mass firings of federal workers.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KRON4

item.title: Trump to resume detaining migrant families in Texas facilities
item.description: President Donald Trump will resume detaining migrant families as part of his administration's mass deportation effort
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Family on cruise ship gets lost luggage back after weeks of waiting, but half of their personal items are missing
item.description: Frustrations continue for Jason and Tammy Merritt after waiting a month for their lost luggage, which ended up being on-board the Carnival Cruise Line the entire time.
item.dc:creator: Mecca Thompson/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cheetos bags full of meth found at post office
item.description: Two Los Angeles residents have been arrested in southern Indiana after police found pounds of meth hidden inside Cheetos bags at a local post office.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elementary student found unharmed after escaping school
item.description: " I just jumped out of the car and tried to grab him to make sure that there was nobody that actually would hit him," Kohler said.
item.dc:creator: Cheyenne Pagan, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Florida residents record SpaceX explosion
item.description: Residents in Tampa Bay, Florida were confounded after seeing what seemed to be an explosion in the sky around Thursday's sunset.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Judge says plans to release a woman in Slender Man case can go forward
item.description: WAUKESHA, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin woman who nearly killed her classmate years ago to please horror character Slender Man can be released from a psychiatric hospital as planned, a judge decided Thursday, rejecting state health officials’ last-minute attempt to keep her committed.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 90s band Spin Doctors' equipment stolen from Pennsylvania storage unit
item.description: Pennsylvania State Police stated that on February 22, troopers began investigating the theft of music equipment from a storage unit at D.M.D Mini Storage in Penn Township, Snyder County.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Muniz, Fox 5

item.title: Experts and recovery teams hope to reach site of Alaska avalanche that buried 3 skiers
item.description: GIRDWOOD, Alaska (AP) — Authorities in Alaska said they would attempt Thursday to reach the site of an avalanche that trapped three skiers, who were believed to have died after being buried in snow.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspects in Kansas City Chiefs fans' deaths plead not guilty
item.description: Jordan Willis and Ivory J. Carson pleaded not guilty during their arraignment in connection with the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: San Carlos Apache teenager's death reverberates throughout Indian Country
item.description: A San Carlos Apache teen who was reported missing and then found dead marks one of the latest tragedies as Native American communities continue to grapple with high rates of people being killed or going missing.
item.dc:creator: Sejal Govindarao, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump stresses Cabinet secretaries lead agency cuts, but keeps Musk as threat
item.description: President Trump on Thursday stressed that his Cabinet secretaries take the lead on making cuts to their agencies and staff, insisting that officials are empowered to make decisions before Elon Musk can step in. “I want the Cabinet members, go first, keep all the people you want.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: These national parks saw record attendance in 2024, but they may not tell you about it
item.description: The National Park Service has reportedly been instructed not to draw attention to the record attendance amid staffing cuts across the agency.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: As DOGE slashes spending, it's missing millions of dollars hidden in plain sight
item.description: As DOGE looks for efficiencies, it may need to start with the government’s own money sitting unclaimed in many states, held by the government.
item.dc:creator: Peter Wilgoren, KTLA 5

item.title: Car being pulled from an Oregon river might have belonged to a family that vanished in 1958
item.description: Authorities said Thursday that they planned to pull a station wagon from an Oregon river believed to have belonged to a family of five that disappeared nearly 70 years ago while out searching for Christmas greenery.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cockpit recorder likely hadn't worked 'for several years' before jet crashed in Philadelphia: NTSB
item.description: The cockpit voice recorder in the medical transport jet that slammed into a Philadelphia neighborhood in January had likely not worked for years, according to a preliminary report released by the National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Andrew Tate says he plans to live in Florida despite 'hee haw' over return to US soil amid overseas charges
item.description: Andrew Tate says he plans to buy a Florida penthouse and live in the United States as he awaits trial in Romania on sex trafficking and other charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Businesses scramble to contain fallout from Trump's tariffs on Canada, China and Mexico
item.description: A Minnesota farmer worries about the price of fertilizer. A San Diego entrepreneur deals with an unexpected cost increase of remodeling a restaurant. A Midwestern sheet metal fabricator bemoans the prospect of higher aluminum prices.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Derek Chauvin pardon 'would set us back as a society': Minnesota AG
item.description: Minnesota’s attorney general is pushing back on calls for President Donald Trump to pardon Derek Chauvin, the white police officer convicted of murdering George Floyd in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Chicago VA outreach program shut down amid Department of Veterans Affairs job cuts
item.description: CHICAGO — The Department of Veterans Affairs will cut tens of thousands of jobs and shut down an outreach program that serves veterans in several Chicago wards. Alderman Matt Martin, 47th Ward, says he received a notice from the Chicago's VA office Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WGN 9

item.title: White House reverses course on Education Department executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump will not sign an executive order Thursday asking new Department of Education Secretary Linda McMahon to begin dissolving the department, a White House official confirmed to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man threatens to assassinate Trump, destroy New York with missiles in 911 calls: report
item.description: Justin Blaxton, 34, allegedly threatened to assassinate President Donald Trump and launch missiles to destroy New York in a series of 911 calls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Job cuts last month higher than any February since 2009
item.description: U.S.-based employers cut more jobs last month than in any February in 16 years, according to a new report from outplacement firm Challenger, Gray and Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Pope rests after peaceful night during third week of pneumonia treatment
item.description: Pope Francis was resting Thursday after a peaceful night during his third week of hospital treatment for double pneumonia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Google feature flags when you might be getting scammed in real time
item.description: Google is rolling out new, AI-powered tools to help stop scammers in their tracks, before they get your data or money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: As many top Democrats stay silent on Cuomo mayoral run, an accuser feels betrayed
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lindsey Boylan helped drive Andrew Cuomo from office in 2021 when the former aide came forward as the first woman to accuse the governor of sexual harassment.
item.dc:creator: BY JAKE OFFENHARTZ and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US paused Ukraine intelligence support: Trump officials
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe and national security adviser Michael Waltz both said the U.S. had paused intelligence sharing in addition to military aid.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
